MedBeacon · Sales Intelligence

SEP-1 Prospect Finder

Hospitals ranked by CMS SEP-1 sepsis-bundle compliance. Lowest scores first — the widest care gap is the strongest reason to buy. Phase columns show where the bundle breaks (severe-sepsis vs septic-shock, 3hr vs 6hr).

Reporting period: Jul 1, 2024 – Jun 30, 2025 · CMS data refresh Q2 2026
EHR qualifier — live: EHR vendor joined in from the ONC CHPL hospital–Promoting Interoperability linkage (latest attestation year). Use the EHR vendor filter to isolate Oracle Health (Cerner) — your Red Rover integration target. Coverage ~89% of reporting hospitals; the rest show as Unknown (typically psychiatric, children's, or non–PI attesters).
Quality & reimbursement — new: Every row now carries the CMS overall star rating (1–5) and the hospital's FY2025 Value-Based Purchasing payment adjustment — the ±% CMS applies to base operating DRG payments. Click any row to expand the SEP-1 phase breakdown, the VBP Total Performance Score with domain scores, and an estimated annual dollar impact. A SEP-1 gap is the clinical story; the VBP penalty is the dollar story — together they make the buying case. Filter to Penalized hospitals to find facilities already losing reimbursement.
Source. CMS Provider Data Catalog — Timely & Effective Care (yv7e-xc69) joined to Hospital General Information (xubh-q36u) on Facility ID.
Period. Jul 1, 2024 – Jun 30, 2025. Snapshot downloaded May 2026; refresh by re-exporting the CSVs (CMS updates quarterly).
SEP-1. Appropriate care for severe sepsis and septic shock. Higher is better; ranked lowest first.
Phase columns. SS-3 / SS-6 = severe sepsis 3hr / 6hr. Shk-3 / Shk-6 = septic shock 3hr / 6hr.
Missing score. Usually low case volume or non-reporting, not poor care — toggle “Reporting only” off to inspect.
Mandate flag. NY, NJ, IL, OH, WI (approximate). Verify current state mandate status before outreach.
EHR vendor. Derived from the ONC CHPL hospital–Promoting Interoperability linkage (hospital-promoting-interoperability-chpl-linkage.csv, healthit.gov). Per facility we take the latest attestation year and pick the inpatient EHR with the most listed certified products, ignoring portals, public-health reporting modules, and direct-messaging utilities. Approximate — verify before outreach.
Star rating. CMS Hospital overall star rating (1–5) from Hospital General Information (xubh-q36u). Shown as “Not rated” where CMS reports no rating (low measure count, specialty, or new facilities).
VBP adjustment. FY2025 Hospital Value-Based Purchasing net payment adjustment factor, IPPS Final Rule Table 16B (CMS-1808-F, posted Dec 2024), keyed by CCN. The factor (~0.98 to ~1.05) multiplies base operating DRG payments; we display it as a signed percent. Applies to ~2,470 IPPS hospitals; non-participants (critical-access, psychiatric, children's, low-volume) show “—”.
Total Performance Score & domains. CMS Hospital VBP — Total Performance Score (ypbt-wvdk), FY2026 refresh. Unweighted domain scores: Clinical Outcomes, Person & Community Engagement, Safety, Efficiency & Cost Reduction (each 0–100).
Estimated annual VBP impact. Estimate, not a CMS figure. Computed as (adjustment factor − 1) × estimated base operating DRG payments, where the base is approximated as 60% of total Medicare inpatient payments (Medicare Inpatient Hospitals by Provider, CY2024). Total Medicare inpatient payment includes capital, IME, DSH, and outlier add-ons that VBP does not touch, so we scale down to the operating-DRG share. Directional — validate against the hospital's CMS Percentage Payment Summary Report before quoting.
